name: SOUL RETREAT: Relax & Meditate in Nature

reviews.review_description:
Still loving it. I just had a really bad day at work (abusive patient), and i couldn't face home and i don't feel like i want to be anywhere, but also i want to be safe. I was able to be on my own in a room with my headset and get away. A place where I was the only one, but also knowing that I'm safe and not worrying family. I spent 1hr on a beach and end up watching the clouds, felt relax after and have put the bad experience in perspective. 
I have an idea that I'm hoping the creators might consider. I love rain, or storm. Would that be possible one day? 
I love the beach and the sun and the night time, but i was also thinking of rain.
Thanks again, for making it possible for me to get away and breathe.

An amazing experience. I’m not really into meditation, but this app made me feel completely immersed, almost like visiting these places in real life. Beautiful atmosphere, natural sounds and deeply relaxing. I highly recommend it to everyone, not just for meditation.

This app has raised the standard for immersion in my opinion. Brink Traveller is another amazing photorealistic app but Soul Retreat has an environment which is dynamic rather than static i.e. water flows, waves lap onto the shoreline, etc. There are some good free environments but i'm going to subscribe for a while to support the devs because this product deserves support.

developer_response.body:
Tyke_18 thank you for this review.

Hearing you say Soul Retreat has raised the standard for immersion really means a lot. We have put a huge amount of effort into making the environments feel alive, with real movement in the water, waves, and wind, because that is such a big part of what makes a place feel present rather than static.

I’m also really glad you called out the free environments, and thank you for choosing to subscribe to support what we are building. I really appreciate it.

Thanks again. Ben

reviews.review_description:
Soul Retreat has become one of my favourite apps on the Meta Quest. The environments are incredibly realistic, beautiful, and immersive - all with natural ambient sounds that genuinely help you relax and disconnect.
I was initially hesitant about the lifetime license due to the small library, but I’ve been pleased to see new high-quality environments added regularly. There’s still room for greater geographic variety and diversity, but the existing ones are outstanding. I hope the collection continues to grow.
What truly sets it apart is the ability to ground meditations in your own religious or spiritual tradition. While most apps remain strictly secular, Soul Retreat respectfully supports integrating personal faith, adding meaningful depth.
Overall, it’s a peaceful, high-quality VR experience. Highly recommended.

developer_response.body:
NakaginPhil thank you for this thoughtful review.

I’m really glad the environments and ambient sounds are helping you relax and switch off. We have put a huge amount of effort into making these places feel genuinely real in the headset, so it means a lot to hear that.

I also really appreciate you mentioning the way Soul Retreat can be personalised around what matters to you. Making the guidance feel more personal, rather than one size fits all, was something I cared a lot about.

And yes, I completely agree on wanting more environment variety over time. We are going to keep adding new high quality environments, and broadening the range.  In fact I’m going to be travelling to capture more environments during the next few weeks.

Thanks again for the recommendation. Ben

reviews.review_description: It’s truly an amazing experience. I have one suggestion. From what I’ve noticed, there seems to be a lot of water environments like rivers and oceans. So I was wondering are there any plans to create other types of environments as well, such as forests, high mountains, or rainy settings?

developer_response.body:
Efertium thank you for this review.

I’m really glad you are finding it such a realistic and relaxing experience.

And yes, absolutely. Locations in forests and views from mountains are all things we plan for future updates. I also intend to investigate the possibility of rain in future and see if it is possible to do with a high enough degree of realism on current hardware.

If you have a favourite type of forest or mountain landscape, feel free to share it on our Discord as that kind of feedback really helps shape updates.
https://discord.gg/zSJQfxaEdk

Thanks again. Ben

reviews.review_title: The most photorealistic scenery on quest
reviews.review_description:
Wow what a surprise. The quality of the scenery in the app is best I've seen on quest 3. Not just a static environment but a full real environment that lasts for few minutes not a quick 10 second looping scenery. 

Have 2 free environments which are great with free one rotating every week. Hope the subscription model doesn't deter people. A environment dlc option instead could be best approach that a lot of other successful apps have used. 

There are a lot of mediation and stories available in app as well or you can just enjoy the nature sounds and the calming waves crashing on the beach.

If you want to showcase the quest 3 to others this is a fantastic that will blow them away.

If we had these environments as our home environments on quest would be fantastic. 

5⭐

developer_response.body:
CrimsonSky3000 thank you so much for this.

Hearing you call it the most photorealistic scenery you have seen on Quest 3 means a lot. We have put a huge amount of effort into capturing real places and making them feel alive.

I’m also really glad you noticed the free version setup, two core environments plus a third that rotates weekly. I genuinely welcome and appreciate free users who use the app regularly. I want this to be something for everyone in the Quest community when they need a place to relax and breathe for a bit.

On pricing, I hear you. I’m trying to balance giving people a genuinely useful free tier, while also funding regular capture trips for new real world locations and the ongoing costs behind the AI guidance. I’m definitely open to exploring different options over time, and feedback like this helps.

Thanks again for the 5 stars and the thoughtful feedback. Ben

reviews.review_description: I absolutely love this app. The nature scenes are breathtaking and the guided meditations are personalized. It's affordable too. I really appreciate Soul Retreat and highly recommend it.

developer_response.body:
WavyPurple12 thank you for this review.
I’m really glad you are enjoying the nature scenes and the personalised meditations. A lot of care has gone into making the environments feel beautiful and the guidance feel more personal, so it means a lot to hear that.
And I’m really glad it feels worth it to you. Thank you as well for the recommendation. Ben

reviews.review_description:
Just using the free version which gives a new environment each week while deleting the old. I just use this about once a week so it’s ok with me. The environments are very good and the blend of 3D with video of waves etc is quite convincing. 
Very relaxing and beautiful. 
I can’t justify the high price of the paid version, but would pay about 15 dollars or so for it plus maybe a dlc or two for new environments.  Best of luck to the developers who definitely have a great product and I’m sure it would be worth it for some people.

developer_response.body:
UnivacOne thanks for this review.

I’m really glad you are enjoying the free version, and I really appreciate you helping spread the word that it has real value even without subscribing. I genuinely welcome free users, especially people who come back each week for the new environment.

Also really glad the blend of 3D and real movement in the waves felt convincing for you. That sense of presence is something we have worked very hard on.

And no problem at all on the paid version not being right for you. I appreciate you trying it, and I’m very glad you feel there is a great product here.

Thanks again, Ben

reviews.review_description:
When i said I wanted a 10 mins guided meditation, the feedback will say yes. But i would still get a 5 mins meditation only. Any help before I cancel my subscription?  Thanks

My question was answered. I changed my review to 5 stars! And chose the Lifetime membership.

Thank you Ben!

developer_response.body:
BubblyEagle699 thank you so much for updating your review, and thank you as well for choosing Lifetime. I really appreciate it, and I’m very glad we were able to get this sorted out for you.

I have also now fixed this properly in the latest beta, so asking by voice for a meditation or story of a particular length works as expected. That will be released more widely soon.

And thank you for getting in touch rather than just giving up on it. If anyone else runs into an issue or has feedback, please do email me at support@soulretreat.app
 or join our Discord. I really do read and respond, and it helps me improve Soul Retreat quickly.
https://discord.gg/zSJQfxaEdk

Thanks again for giving it another go and for taking the time to update your review. Ben

reviews.review_title: Even a Non-Meditator Can Relax Here
reviews.review_description:
I’m not usually a meditation person, but Soul Retreat won me over with how real it feels. The environments look like you could step into them, and the nature sounds are so convincing that my brain forgets it’s in a headset and starts behaving like it’s actually near water and open sky. The visuals aren’t just pretty, they’re calming in that quiet, effortless way that makes it easy to just sit there and breathe without feeling like you’re “doing meditation homework.” With Meta’s setting to play an app while lying down, this app has put me to sleep….so relaxing.   

I also really like the personal touches, especially being able to choose a male or female voice for guidance and switch into story modes when I don’t want straight-up meditation. It makes the experience feel more like a relaxing escape than a strict wellness routine. Even if you’re normally allergic to meditation apps, Soul Retreat’s mix of photoreal scenery, soothing audio, and flexible guidance makes it surprisingly easy to stick around. 

The app is free so might as well give it a try. If you really really like it and want more of retreating your soul, you can upgrade with two options available: subscription or lifetime purchase; or just keep using the free version.

developer_response.body:
MissChiefVR this made my day to read, thank you.

I love that you called out the feeling of your brain forgetting it is in a headset. That is exactly what we have been chasing with Soul Retreat. We have put a massive amount of effort into making these places feel real, from the grounded presence technique to my friend Ignacio hiking for hours with heavy capture gear to bring back the best possible nature footage and audio.

Also very happy the lying down mode is working for you. Hearing that it actually helped you fall asleep is honestly one of the best outcomes I could hope for.

And thanks for noticing the little flexibility things like voice choice and story mode. My goal has been to make this feel like an escape you can return to, not meditation homework.

Really appreciate you taking the time to write such a thoughtful review, and thanks for recommending the free version too. Ben

reviews.review_description: I have a lot of pain issues due to disability so can get stressed and anxiety ridden...this app alongside the maloka app have controlled my anxiety levels and made night time relaxing. The body meditation takes stress out my body which helps reduce pain levels,I subscribed for a year and feel it is well worth the money,it's not going to be for everyone but I personally found it relieves anxiety if you do a 15 min session every night...I would really love many more environments,maybe concentrating on a wider part of the world,a waterfall,mountains,etc might be an idea.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to write this.

I’m really glad Soul Retreat is helping you relax at night and helping with your anxiety. Hearing that the body meditation is helping release stress from your body and making evenings easier genuinely means a lot to me.

And yes, we are planning to add more environments over the coming months, including mountains and waterfalls.

Thanks again for the thoughtful review. Ben

reviews.review_description:
Decided to check this out and I'll first say the lifetime license price is beyond ridiculous. Unless 100+ areas are going to be added, there is no way this would be worth the lifetime price.

As for the base app, the quality of the two areas is worse than the photogrammetry that is used in Real VR Fishing as the scenery is fairly blurry, even when increasing the render resolution to 3k vertical per eye which is 40% higher than default. I also can't believe the frame/refresh rate is at 72Hz as though this is for the Quest 2 and not the Quest 3/3s. I checked out the data in the OVR metrics tool and it is barely using the gpu and cpu (set to level 2 for each) so there is no reason why it doesn't default to 90Hz/fps. Same with the render resolution which can be set a lot higher. There is a lot of untapped performance that could and should be getting used here. 72Hz causes eye strain and headaches for some of us in VR so that is why I just shake my head at it being used here. 

I also didn't care for the apparent AI use for the narration. I heard the female voice mispronounce a simple word.

developer_response.body:
loregnum thanks again for the detailed feedback. Quick note after the v0.2.29 update - the free version is clearer and has more options from the start. It includes two full environments plus a third bonus environment that changes weekly, and after your first session there are no mentions of Plus apart from a small [+] button. I genuinely welcome people using it free.

On pricing, totally fair to dislike subscriptions or lifetime. The paid options are aimed at people who want to use Soul Retreat regularly to de stress and wind down in the evenings. The paid tier is what funds ongoing capture of new real world locations and the ongoing server costs of adaptive guidance.

On visuals, Soul Retreat uses real world 360 capture with a grounded 6DoF approach so you can move naturally within your room, with lots of nearfield motion like waves and flowing water. That focus means it does not behave like photogrammetry, and cranking render resolution with external tools does not always translate into the same kind of sharpness you might expect. I will be working on ways to increase graphical detail and realism further in future updates.

On 72Hz, I have prioritised stability, comfort, and battery drain for long, calm sessions, in line with Meta’s requirements. I have not had any reports of comfort issues from 72Hz so far, and my understanding is that higher refresh rates are most helpful when there is artificial locomotion or very fast movement, neither of which is likely to happen in a meditation app like this.

On the narration, sorry about the mispronunciation. If you are up for it, email me at support at soulretreat.app with the environment you tested most and the word that was mispronounced. I will look into it. Ben

reviews.review_description: Soul Retreat is an absolutely beautiful and calming experience on the Quest. The environments feel incredibly real — from the subtle movement of nature to the immersive design, it genuinely feels like stepping into a peaceful place rather than just putting on a headset. The sense of presence is amazing. I’m very excited to see how this app continues to develop — there’s so much potential here. Highly recommended for anyone looking to unwind and reset.

developer_response.body:
svalurthor thank you so much for this.

I’m really glad the realism and sense of presence came through for you, especially the subtle movement in the scene. That is exactly what we have been aiming for, to make it feel like stepping into a peaceful place rather than just putting on a headset.

And thanks for the encouragement about the future. We are going to keep building it out with new environments and improvements over time.

Really appreciate the recommendation. Ben

reviews.review_title: Nice relaxing app after a stressful day.
reviews.review_description:
I'm not into meditation, but sometimes I just want to chill and relax and take in same great scenery, and the AI narrator doesn't sound robotic to the point where it takes you out of the immersion. 
The free version of the app is generous with 2 environments and how it rotates a different environment every week, though you can sub monthly, yearly, or opt for lifetime sub as well. .

developer_response.body:
Gruntled thanks for this review.

I’m really glad Soul Retreat works for you even without being into meditation. A big part of the goal is exactly that, somewhere you can just chill, breathe a bit, and take in beautiful scenery after a stressful day.

And I’m very happy you called out the AI voice not breaking immersion, and that the free version feels generous with the two included environments plus another that rotates each week. I genuinely welcome people using the free version regularly.

Thanks again. Ben

reviews.review_title: One of the best meditation app out
reviews.review_description:
I have a lot of meditation apps and this one looks the best. Some of the other ones will add cgi animals or have artificial looking water movement which breaks immersion. 

I took off one star because the lifetime pass is just insane. I'd love to support the developers but not spend a fortune doing it and im so over monthly subscriptions. That being said the free package with a new environment is fair. 

As long as they occasionally add new environments I'll continue to use it.

developer_response.body:
ChirpChum thanks so much for this, and I’m really glad the realism is coming through for you. We have worked hard to keep the water and nearfield movement natural, because that is exactly the sort of thing that can break immersion if it looks artificial.

I’m also really glad you feel the free version is fair, with two environments included plus another that changes each week.

On pricing, I totally hear you. Many people are not keen on subscriptions, and lifetime is a big ask. I’m thinking carefully about how to offer a range of pricing options that work for different people, while still bringing in enough to fund regular capture trips for new real world environments, plus the ongoing server costs behind the adaptive guidance.

And yes, we do plan to keep adding new environments. Thanks again for using it, and for the honest review. Ben

reviews.review_title: Treat Yo’Self: A Soul Retreat Review
reviews.review_description:
Full disclosure: I received a copy of this game. 

Overview: Life is busy and demanding, often it feels like we never make any time for ourselves. This app aims to change that. With photo realistic, immersive environments, calming nature sounds, and a relaxing (AI) narrator, Soul Retreat is a well designed app to help you relax and reflect after a long day. 

Positives:

+ Great Immersive Environments
+ Calming Nature Sounds 
+ Relaxing Narration
+ Hand Tracking Option
+ Customizable to Night or Day Settings
+ Doesn’t require Internet - Travels Well

Negatives:

- AI Narration (though realistically it would be cost-prohibitive for this app to constantly have someone on hand to record new phrases)
- Menus, while functional, feel a little dated in design (personal opinion)

Final review: I used this app for over two months and found that it was more effective at helping me to relax and fall asleep than anything else. It is also helpful if you are getting into meditation and wish to do so in settings that may not be available to you where you live. ￼￼ A well-deserved 5-star rating.

developer_response.body:
BoardGameNick thanks for taking the time to write such a thoughtful review.

Just to clarify for anyone reading, the free copy was through beta testing, with no requirement to leave a review. That early feedback genuinely helped improve the app, so I really appreciate it.

I’m really glad Soul Retreat helped you relax and fall asleep more effectively than anything else. That is exactly the kind of use I built it for, to be something people can turn to at the end of a long day when they need a bit of calm.

I also appreciate the honest notes on the narration and menu design. I’m continuing to refine both, and that kind of feedback is useful.

Thanks again for the review. Ben

reviews.review_description:
➡️ Le tres bon point, ce sont les paysages, souvent au bord de l'eau, et le son.
Perso j'adore, ca me relaxe direct, et c'est un peu ce que j'ai vu de mieux dans le genre.

➡️ L'autre bon point est l'IA : on peut la regler dans notre langue dans les parametres, et avec un clic sur <A> on peut lui demander ce qu'on veut.

➡️ Sinon vous pouvez aussi passer par le menu pour choisir une seance de meditation sur un theme, ou une histoire ou juste de la musique.

➡️ Le principal reproche est l'UI et aussi l'UX : je trouve toujours cela tres confus. N'hésitez donc pas à explorer les menus pour voir un peu tout ce qui est possible.

➡️ A noter que le bouton Menu du Quest (bouton plat manette gauche) n'est pas utilisé, mais le bouton B appelera le menu.

➡️ Notez aussi que quand vous choisissez une séance, pendant quelques secondes/minutes, l'IA va vous reparler en anglais : pas de panique, elle va reprendre ensuite dans votre langue quand la séance va vraiment démarrer.

➡️ N'hésitez pas à tester la version gratuite, on peut choisir ensuite si on veut d'acheter ou de prendre un abonnement.

developer_response.body:
Je lis votre avis via une traduction automatique, donc désolé si certaines tournures sont un peu maladroites.

Merci beaucoup kaak99 pour cet avis très détaillé. Je suis vraiment content que les paysages au bord de l’eau et les sons de la nature vous détendent aussi rapidement, et que vous trouviez que c’est parmi ce qui se fait de mieux dans le genre.

Merci pour votre retour sur l’UI et l’UX. Je vais continuer à travailler pour rendre le système de menus plus simple au fil des semaines et des mois. Et concernant le bouton Menu du Quest, j’ai noté qu’il fallait le brancher pour qu’il ouvre aussi le menu, comme le bouton B.

Juste pour clarifier pour ceux qui lisent, la possibilité de parler à votre guide IA est une fonctionnalité disponible dans Soul Retreat Plus. Et la seule langue officiellement prise en charge pour le moment est l’anglais. La version actuelle en live n’a pas d’option dans le menu pour régler la langue, mais je vais réintroduire prochainement la possibilité de changer de langue en le demandant à la voix dans une mise à jour. C’est une fonctionnalité expérimentale pour l’instant et cela ne change pas la langue des menus ni les phrases fixes.

Merci encore de recommander la version gratuite. Ben

reviews.review_title: The Best Way to Find Stillness in VR
reviews.review_description:
I ​have been a tester for Soul Retreat since its early stages. With the official launch version, the team has truly stuck the landing. The UI is clean, the performance is buttery smooth, and the entire experience feels incredibly grounded and purposeful.

You may compare it to ​ to Brink Traveler, and while Brink is fantastic for virtual tourism and sharp geometric detail, the two apps have very different souls. Brink can feel a bit static—like standing in a beautiful, high-res postcard. Soul Retreat, on the other hand, is all about immersion through motion. The magic is in the details right at your feet: waves rolling onto the shore, rivers flowing with realistic momentum, and lakes rippling in the breeze. That constant, natural movement makes the 6DoF feel much more "real" and adds a layer of presence that Brink just doesn't hit. It’s less about sightseeing and more about settling in.

​It has become my go-to "reset button." Whether I need a 5-minute breathing break between meetings or a longer, guided meditation to decompress after a long day, it works flawlessly without any friction.

​If you’re looking for a quiet, focused space to actually de-stress rather than just look at rocks, Soul Retreat has earned its permanent spot in my library.

developer_response.body:
SigmaBlaze thank you so much for this, and also thank you for being part of the early testing.

I really appreciate how you described the difference in intent. Soul Retreat is not trying to be a virtual tourism app, it is trying to be a place you can actually settle into and reset. That nearfield motion, waves at your feet, water moving with real momentum, grass and wind, is exactly the stuff we have obsessed over because it is what helps your brain feel present.

And your reset button line was genuinely great to read. That is exactly what we are aiming for, something you can use for a quick breathing break or a longer wind down, and it just works without fuss.

Also really happy you called out the UI and performance. The beta group feedback made a huge difference there, and I’m grateful for it.

Thanks again for the thoughtful comparison and the recommendation. Ben

reviews.review_title: The environments are gorgeous, try it!!
reviews.review_description:
I am someone who has struggled to meditate for years. I have ADHD, and sitting down to meditate without entertainment or distraction is *really* hard. Soul Retreat has had me meditating x3 per week for nearly three months now, and it's the first time I've been able to meditate regularly in about 15 years.

First of all, the environments are gorgeous, both graphically and in terms of the scenery. I legitimately feel emotionally connected to some of the environments ('Portalet' feels like Aloy or Kratos from God of War could come journeying through at any moment), and putting on Soul Retreat to meditate in these environments now feels like visiting a comforting, familiar place. I don't know how, but these photo-real environments have 6DoF and roomscale movement, which is amazing! It makes it feels like I'm really in these real places. When I sit down or lean forwards, I move through the world, instead of it just being a fixed, immovable 360 degree video anchored to my face. This ease of movement within the spaces adds to the relaxation.

I should also note there is a wide variety of environments to match different people's needs. Warm beaches, soothing mountain ponds and streams, etc. I have to mention, you can manually dim environments to be midday, evening, sunset, night, etc, and it really helps set the vibe you want.

The audio of all the environments is also super chill and relaxing!

The AI guide (and preferences menu) tailors meditations to your tastes based upon your personal spirituality (it also includes athiesm, thankfully) and tastes, which helps it meet my needs. You can also choose a male or female voice, which I like. (General safety note: remember that it is not safe to treat any form of AI as a therapist - this warning is just me being cautious and making sure people know not to do that. Soul Retreat is in no way trying to be an AI therapist, to be clear, so this is not a negative point about the app!). 

Part of what has kept me coming back is the "My Journey" tracker. It tracks how many meditations you've done in a week, and how many weeks you've had of three or more meditations. This really helps me see my progress, and is a fantastic motivator!

If you don't want a guided meditation, you can also let it tell you stories or just listen to calming music in a beautiful place.

I cannot recommend this app highly enough! At the very least, try out the free version! You get two environments, plus a third that changes weekly. There's also a free trial for the full version that gets you all 10 environments, and if you don't want to subscribe you can also just pay a single, one-time fee for lifetime access. I DID playtest this app, so I got for free, but I am very happy to recommend it!

Also, the developer has been so responsive throughout the months of playtesting, it's clear they really care! I suspect this app will just keep getting better and better.

developer_response.body:
The CinemaNaut thank you so much for this.

I love what you said about feeling emotionally connected to certain places, especially Portalet. That feeling of a comforting, familiar place is exactly what I am trying to build, and it is why we have put such a massive amount of effort into making the environments feel real and grounded in 6DoF.

Also really glad the time of day controls and the audio are working for you. Those little vibe details matter so much for winding down.

Thank you for the thoughtful note about the AI too. I completely agree. The guide is there to support relaxation and personalised sessions, not to replace professional help, and I’m glad you called that out.

And the My Journey shout out means a lot. I built that because I wanted something gently motivating without making it feel like a chore.

Also thanks for being part of the Impact Labs beta test. Having a beta group has made a huge difference to getting the launch build into shape, and I really appreciate you taking the time to share feedback during that process.

Seriously, thank you again for such a detailed review, and for the kind words about how responsive I have been. I really do care, and I am going to keep improving it with the community. Ben

reviews.review_title: The app I was waiting for
reviews.review_description: I was looking for ages for a VR app where I could just go to be in a peaceful place. There are others that are quite relaxing but you're very aware that you're in an app. Sitting by the stream in soul retreat is almost as good as the real thing. The meditations make it even better. Love it thank you.

developer_response.body:
Robbunt thank you so much.

I love that you described it as a place you can just go to, and that sitting by the stream feels almost as good as the real thing. That is exactly what I have been trying to build. Really glad the meditations add to it for you too. Ben

reviews.review_description: Soul Retreat is a calming VR meditation app that places you in photorealistic natural environments designed to help you relax, focus, and unwind. Using room-scale movement and realistic nature sounds, the experience feels immersive rather than like a simple 360° video. The app offers guided meditations, breathing exercises, sleep stories, and calming music, with optional AI guidance that can personalize sessions. Overall, Soul Retreat is a great VR escape for relaxation and mindfulness, especially if you enjoy nature environments and guided meditation.

developer_response.body:
FoamyMouthDog thanks for this review.

I’m really glad you highlighted the feeling of being immersed in a real place rather than just watching a simple 360 video. That sense of presence is something we have worked incredibly hard on.

And I’m very happy the mix of guided meditations, breathing, stories, music, and optional AI guidance is resonating with you too.

If you have any thoughts on how I could make Soul Retreat even better, I’d genuinely love to hear them. We have a Discord where I collect feedback which helps shape updates.
https://discord.gg/zSJQfxaEdk

Thanks again. Ben

reviews.review_title: Helped When I Was In Pain
reviews.review_description:
Soul Retreat is relaxing without feeling too forced or artificial. I have briefly tried other meditation apps, but they felt abstract and uninteresting. The natural scenes in Soul Retreat are perfect for putting you in the right frame of mind. I also liked using the stories and history; they aren't really all that engaging or informative, but they give you just enough engagement to help keep you there in the moment.

I have not used meditation much, so this was an experiment for me. I developed a herniated disk in my neck that pinched a nerve in my arm. Laying down hurt except in very specific positions and I had a lot of trouble sleeping. There were some middle of the night relaxation sessions that really helped. It probably will not become a normal part of my routine. I normally sleep easily and I think I shed stress easily, but I would still want to have an app like this for occasional use. I hope that I will not need this app much in the long term, but I can't deny that it has provided some needed relaxation and distraction during some painful periods.

developer_response.body:
tpancoast thanks for taking the time to write this, and I’m really sorry you have been dealing with that.

I’m really glad Soul Retreat helped you during a painful period. Thank you for sharing that. I’m building this to be something people can lean on when life is difficult, so it really means a lot to hear it made a difference for you.

Also appreciate the honest note on the stories and history. That is pretty much the balance I am aiming for, just enough engagement to keep you present, without it feeling like you have to focus hard.

Wishing you a smooth recovery, and thank you again for the thoughtful review. Ben

reviews.review_description:
I never really clicked with meditation before, and I didn’t expect VR to change that. This was easy to settle into. The real video environments and calming sounds helped me relax without trying too hard, and the guidance didn’t feel forced.

What really stood out to me is how it affected me after I was done. I noticed I felt calmer and handled things better throughout the day, even stuff that would normally stress me out. I didn’t expect it to carry over into my workday, but it does. I’m noticeably more relaxed at work and find myself mentally returning to the lessons from the sessions to help me deal with work stress. That relaxed feeling stuck around longer than I expected.

I also like that I can really zone out and get completely relaxed without worrying about losing track of time. That has happened more than I want to admit. The session timer allows the guide to gently bring me back so I can manage my time better, in case the session is a little too effective and I fall asleep in the headset.

developer_response.body:
Angelfire340 thanks so much for writing this.

I am really glad it was easy to settle into, especially given meditation has not clicked for you in the past. That is exactly what I am aiming for, something that feels natural and calming rather than forced.

What you said about it carrying over into your day genuinely means a lot to me. I quit my job because I wanted to make something that could make a real difference for people, and it is incredible hearing that Soul Retreat is doing that for you. It makes all the work feel worthwhile.

And it is also amazing to hear it has even put you to sleep in the headset. That is kind of the ultimate sign you have properly switched off, and I am really happy it is helping you unwind that much.

And yes, the timer is there for exactly that reason. I wanted a gentle way to bring you back so you can relax without worrying you will lose track of time.

Thanks again for taking the time to share this. Ben

reviews.review_description: This app has provided the easiest and best meditation sessions of my life.  I have had times when I was moved to tears.  Normally I do not care for digital assists for this sort of thing, but this one I look forward to coming back to.  It offers a great place to go and relax even if just for a few minutes.  There are a good number of customization options to adjust to your liking.  With much more I could say, just give it a try for free.  I never expected to enjoy it as much as I have.

developer_response.body:
DirkBuckler thank you so much for this.

Hearing that it has given you the easiest and best meditation sessions you have ever had genuinely means a lot to me. And the fact it has moved you that deeply is incredible to hear.

I also really appreciate you saying you normally do not care for digital assistance but you still look forward to coming back. That is exactly what I am trying to build, a place you can drop into for a few minutes and it just helps you settle.

Thanks as well for recommending the free version, and for taking the time to write this. Ben

reviews.review_title: Relax by the sea side or the mountains
reviews.review_description: Soul Retreat takes you to beautiful photogrammetry locations for guided meditation where you can select from different styles of meditation like breathing, affirmations, stories and movement.  My favorites are the story meditation particularly the nature stories read by my AI guide Orion, he has a very calming voice, the stories are related to the location you are in so selecting a different environment will generate a different story.  I look forward to my session each night to relax before bed, the heart rate monitor on my watch shows this does work.  Between the calming story, calming music the sound of moving water and breathing this has helped me relax and sleep better.  There are so many options to tailor your session to what works best for you and you can always ask your AI guide to pick something helpful for how you are feeling and your goals.

developer_response.body:
Ellehcim99 thank you so much for writing this.

I am really glad the Orion mindfulness guide is working for you. Getting the voice to feel calming and supportive without sounding robotic has taken a ton of iteration, and feedback like this helps a lot.

I also love that you mentioned the stories being tied to the place you are in. That was a big goal for me, because if you feel connected to the environment it stops feeling like generic meditation audio and starts feeling like you are actually there, winding down by the sea or in the mountains.

And the watch heart rate note is amazing to hear. I know everyone is different, but hearing it is showing up in a real measurable way is very motivating.

Thanks again for making Soul Retreat part of your nightly routine. It means a lot. Ben

reviews.review_description:
Pretty darn cool!!  Absolutely the best dynamic environment I’ve seen on Quest 3.  Overall ran smooth with very few graphical defects.

I sat down and took it all in and felt nice and relaxed!  Then I had the idea to try running on my treadmill while in the app and that was pretty cool too.  If there was a way to scan in items from your room such as a couch, treadmill, etc. that would be awesome!

developer_response.body:
TakeCare-Bye thanks so much for this, and I am really glad it ran smooth for you.

Hearing you call it the best dynamic environment you have seen on Quest 3 means a lot! We have gone to pretty extreme lengths to make these places feel alive and real, and it is always nice to hear that it is working for someone who is not inside my own biased dev bubble.

The treadmill use case is cool. I love that you experimented with it and found it relaxing in a different way.

On scanning items from your room, some kind of mixed reality passthrough mode is something I'm actively considering.  It might be challenging to do it all with the compute budget of the Quest 3, but I'd like to try and I can see that it would be useful in cases where people don't want to be 100% immersed.  I just have to balance this vs the other improvements I want to make and am definitely listening to the community on this.  

If you are up for it, come join our Discord. It is the best place to share feature ideas like this and help shape what I build next.
https://discord.gg/zSJQfxaEdk

Thanks again for trying it and for the thoughtful suggestion. Ben

reviews.review_description:
Loved it from the start, love the layouts, the environments,  the helpful relaxation tips. 

The only things I would like changed/updated is the speed at which you can move around and free movement to look around or change direction. Other than that, this is one of my favourite apps to play.

developer_response.body:
OzAlien thanks so much for this, I’m really glad you loved it from the start.

And thank you for the movement feedback. At the moment Soul Retreat is designed around room scale movement, so you can move naturally within the space you have, but it does not support free roaming beyond your room. That said, I completely get what you mean about changing direction more comfortably, and I will look at adding a smooth turn option in a future update.

Really appreciate the support, and I’m really happy it is one of your favourite apps. Ben

reviews.review_description:
This is the only app I've ever been willing to get a subscription for because these environments are truly magnificent!   it's a magical experience and I get lost in the beauty and realism of each and every environment they have developed!    truly truly a magnificent app!

although i love the voice options and personalization -- I sometimes just play music through the browser while I sit and enjoy the scenery which is very unusual for my ADHD brain to not get bored and restless.

developer_response.body:
JumboBadger1264 thank you so much for this.

Hearing you say the environments are magnificent and that you were willing to subscribe because of them is amazing. We have put a huge amount of effort into capturing real places and making them feel properly there in the headset, so I’m really glad it is coming through for you.

And I love that you mentioned using the browser for your own music while you sit in the scenery. That is exactly the kind of flexible use I hoped people would discover, and it is especially nice to hear it helps even when your brain wants to get bored and restless.

Thanks again for supporting the app and for the lovely words. Ben

reviews.review_title: I'm not into meditation, but enjoyable
reviews.review_description: I'm not someone who would go out of my way to meditate, but I tried this app out for more than 3 months and found it very enjoyable.  The AI-generated meditations based on history, nature, and legends were interesting but just the calming nature environments on their own had quite a bit of benefit.  I don't expect to use the app every day, but any time I'm feeling stress it provides a nice escape.  The various available environments allow me to change things up based on how I'm feeling on a particular day and along with the changing meditations keep things from getting boring.

developer_response.body:
Mepper thanks so much for this.

I love that you are not really into meditation but still found it genuinely helpful. That is exactly why I built it around real nature, so even if you skip guidance you can just sit somewhere beautiful for a few minutes and let things settle.

Also really glad you enjoyed the history, nature, and legends themes. Keeping it varied and not repetitive has been a big goal.  And thanks for sticking with it since the early days, that kind of feedback helped a lot.

Thanks Ben

reviews.review_description:
TLDR: This is the only app I make time for multiple times each week, with benefits that carry over into real life long after I power down.

I had the chance to preview the full-access version of Soul Retreat, and it’s become a reliable place I return to when I need to slow down and feel more like myself.  If you’re unsure whether it’s for you, the free version is risk-free to try, and spending time with the rotating environments gives a real sense of how it might fit into your routine.

I’ve been meditating near-daily for decades, and I was quite surprised by how much this added to my practice.  With full access, I can use the conversational AI to talk through what’s actually coming up for me, including generalized stress, anxiety, or something weighing on me that day, and let the session respond in a way that helps me slow my breathing, lower my shoulders, relieve tension, and feel grounded.  It isn't therapy, but it is certainly therapeutic for me.  I'm not usually a fan of AI, but here it effectively tunes my sessions for maximum results.

The environments feel realistic and calming, fully immersive with no bad views and semi-spatial audio.  While I can’t freely walk around, I can rotate completely and adjust my distance from the water, and I often recline in my chair to maximize comfort.  I can also set the time of day for any environment, from bright mid-day sunlight to a softer evening sunset, and even paint star patterns in the sky with a stroke of my hand, which is especially beautiful in the nighttime setting.

Before jumping into sessions, I recommend spending a little time with the tutorials to learn the gestures for hand-tracking or controller use as they may not be as intuitive at first.  Then explore the Preferences menus so the app can get to know you.  You can set how you carry stress, the type of mentorship or guidance you prefer, whether you want religious, spiritual, or secular approaches, plus many other personal preferences.  Environmental volume and session length are in there too.  All of this helps me tailor sessions in meaningful, personal, and effective ways.

I can meditate with guidance, sit in silence, add music, or even open the Meta browser to read or listen to an audiobook while pacing my breath to the waves or river flow.  The sessions are flexible enough to return to any time, day or night, and easy to settle into.  There’s even a quick launch feature so you can jump straight into a meditative session or story after completing the initial tutorials.

I can easily recommend it, whether you are an experienced, well-practiced meditator or whether you are new to meditation and are finding it difficult to ground yourself and focus.  We all need 5 (or more) quiet minutes a day to breathe, and this app just makes it a bit easier to do that.

developer_response.body:
muggito wow, thank you for taking the time to write such a thoughtful review.

I really appreciate you being part of the early full access beta, and I am genuinely glad it has become something you come back to several times a week. Hearing it can support an already established practice means a lot.

I also really appreciate how clearly you framed the AI. My intention is that it helps tailor a session and support a calmer routine, but it is not therapy and I never want to present it that way. I am actively improving that feature, and all the feedback you have given me has been incredibly helpful.

And thank you for calling out the tutorials and Preferences. Those menus took a lot of work, and they are one of the big ways Soul Retreat can feel more personal without being complicated.

On movement, just to clarify for anyone reading, you can walk about naturally within your playspace, so you can take a couple of steps and be closer to the water, but it does not allow you to go beyond the space you have in your room.

Also thanks for being active in the Discord. I really do want to keep building this with the community, and having people like you who take the time to share detailed thoughts makes a huge difference.

Ben

reviews.review_title: Soul Retreat now you can really relax
reviews.review_description:
The combination of beautiful 360 video filmed in Scotland combined with gently narrated stories and meditations is an absolute winner.
You can choose from preset audio or tell Soul Retreat what you would like to listen to and it will craft a soundtrack specifically for you.
Amazing ……
If you like guided breathing to help to relax - this is also so easy to follow.

When I want to escape from reality, I jump into soul retrait.

 A place where you don’t have to think about anything. Just listen to the voice (you can choose between male or female), which guide you through some meditation movements / breathings. Or if you just want to listen to a story…
Choose the best environment that you like. On the background the relaxing sounds of the sea or floating water, birds…

Set your timer on how much time you want to escape from reality. And enjoy the moment.

developer_response.body:
POWERGIRL.BE thank you so much for this.

I love how you described it as a place you can drop into and just switch off for a bit. That is exactly what I am trying to build, a calm escape where you do not have to overthink anything.

Really glad you are enjoying the voice choice, the breathing and meditation sessions, and the stories too. And the ambisonic spatial sound is a big part of that sense of realism, with the sea, birds, and water feeling like they are really around you rather than just playing flat audio.

And yes, the timer is there so you can fully relax without worrying you will lose track of time.

Thanks again for the lovely review. Ben

reviews.review_description:
You find yourself in very relaxing, lively natural landscapes, complete with the sounds of nature.
You can also move around a bit.
It's already very relaxing.
If you wish, the AI ​​will give you a meditation session or tell you a story.
The voices are pleasant, there are several variations, and they speak different languages.
I still find the price a bit high, but the free version already gives you access to quite a lot and is usable.

developer_response.body:
propofil thanks so much for the lovely review.

I’m really glad you are enjoying the natural landscapes and the nature sounds, and that the room scale movement adds to the feeling of being there.

Also happy to hear the voices worked for you and that you found the free version properly usable. That is important to me, I genuinely want the free tier to feel worthwhile.

Thanks again for taking the time to write this. Ben

reviews.review_title: Not just 360 video - you can walk around
reviews.review_description:
If you haven’t tried it, you might just assume it’s regular 360 video - but it’s not. You can stand up and your feet are on the ground and take a few steps round your room. It feels really real.

I love the way the music helps me breathe slowly and relax.  I love the affirmation meditations and mentor stories.

developer_response.body:
Marian47 thank you for your review.

I really appreciate you calling out the not simply 360 video point. Communicating that this really is room scale and you can move around with 6DoF is surprisingly hard. The ground being in the right place rather than the weird uncomfortable floating feeling you get with regular 360 video makes a big difference difference to how real it feels.  It's also cool you can take a few steps around or lean down and look at a shell or pebble.

I’m also really glad the breathing music is helping you slow down and relax, and that the affirmations and mentor stories are clicking for you.

Thanks again for the review. Ben

reviews.review_title: I love this app more and more
reviews.review_description:
Update after the developers reply:
I highly appreciate that you are really interested in feedback to further enhance your app. 

At first, I was a bit skeptical and didn't get along well with the app's controls. I highly recommend listening to the tutorials; navigating with your hands becomes much easier afterward. The graphics are truly among the best I've seen on the Quest 3 so far. The locations are beautifully animated, and the customizable meditations are very relaxing. But simply observing the scenery and listening to the flowing water is calming. I wouldn't have thought that after just a few minutes with the app, but I'm liking it more and more. A definite recommendation, especially since a limited version can be tested for free.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to share this, and I’m really glad you enjoyed the visuals. I also hear you on the points you raised, and I’m working hard to keep improving it.

On English only, I completely understand that translating in your head is not relaxing. Right now Soul Retreat only supports English, and that is what the store listing says. Supporting more languages is a bigger job than just translating the AI voice, because the app UI and fixed guidance phrases need doing properly too. I’m a solo developer and it is Early Access, so I have focused on getting a solid English release out first. I am exploring a partial option where the AI voice could support other languages sooner, even if parts of the UI and some fixed phrases stay in English at first. In the meantime, you could try sessions without voice and just use music with slow paced breathing cues. You can select these from the Music tab.

On hands free controls, I’m sorry it felt cumbersome. Reliability is a priority and I’m actively improving it. If you can tell me which actions took multiple attempts, it really helps me reproduce it and fix it.

On the stories, it is very personal. Some people love them and find them calming, others do not. I completely respect your preference. If stories are not relaxing for you, you can stick to the meditations, or just use music sessions and the environment.

If you’re up for it, I’d really value your input by email at support at soulretreat.app. Two quick questions are all I need. Would a partial language option, where most spoken guidance is in your language but the UI and some fixed phrases stay English, be better than English only, or would it just feel frustrating. And which hands free actions were unreliable for you. That feedback genuinely helps me decide what to prioritise next. Thanks again. Ben

reviews.review_description: I love this app way more than I thought I would, have been using it several times a week.  It is by far the best way I have found to help clear the head and just relax.  In the past when I try to take a breather to relax either during the  day or in the evening so I can get to sleep sooner I have had middling results.  Soul Retreat has helped me far more.  Being in a high def immersive naturescape along with the guided voice and almost immediately my mind is gently focused on that and not all the nonsense that clogs it up during the day.  I like the focus on breathing exercises and the stories.  It's the perfect blend of having your brain focus yet not work hard.  It is so peaceful and every time I feel markedly more relaxed.  There are other areas you can choose to focus on too besides just relaxation such as energize a, focus, and others.  If you're looking to regain some calm in your life or want to energize your brain to help focus on work or projects I highly recommend this title.  The scenery with the ocean/lake/river sounds is so beautiful, fells like I'm actually there.

developer_response.body:
Potatochip007 thank you so much for taking the time to write this.

I really love how you described it gently focusing your mind away from the daily noise. That is exactly what I have found too, that being in a high def nature environment can make it much easier to switch off than trying to do the same thing on a phone.

I’m really glad the breathing and stories are working for you, and that you have been using it several times a week. That is the dream I’m pursuing, something you actually come back to because it feels good, not because you feel you should.

And hearing you say it feels like you are actually there by the water means a lot. We have worked really hard on that sense of presence, from the way the environments are captured to the grounded feeling of being able to sit or stand naturally.

Thanks again for the recommendation. Ben

reviews.review_description: I really enjoy the realistic environments. I wish there was more diversity in the environments, such as a green forest, a sandy desert, a snowy mountain. The guided meditation could use some work, the breathing exercises are a little off with their timing. You are supposed to exhale slowly, not all at once. Maybe some kind of light or particle moving to and from to guide the user. The lifetime price is a little less than Tripp, but a lot less content as of now. Do you have plans to regularly update with new environments? ￼

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful review, and I’m really glad you are enjoying the realistic environments.

On variety, we completely agree. More snowy mountains and more trees are on our wish list, and adding more diverse places is a big part of what we want to build next.

On the breathing timing, thanks for calling that out and I will check it. The intention is definitely a gradual exhale, not all at once. There is a breathing sphere that appears during the breathing exercises which expands and contracts at the rate you should breathe in and out. It sounds like perhaps you did not see that, or it did not feel right for you. If you have a moment, please let me know what about the breathing felt off, and which exercise you tried.

On the guided meditations, I would genuinely welcome a bit more detail on what felt off and which styles you tried. If you are up for it, please join our Discord and share your thoughts, the link is in the app under Connect and Support in Profile, or you can email me at support at soulretreat.app.

And yes, we are planning to add new environments regularly, roughly every 4 to 6 weeks. Ben

reviews.review_description:
Soul Retreat is an excellent way to get into meditation in VR.

The free model gives you a great sample of what to expect from the app, including a rotating map.



With its tailored meditation sessions that can fit into your schedule (whether you can just spare 5 mins or 30)

There is a range of options for you to dabble with and a range of focuses you can have your meditation sessions revolve around.



I had never meditated before I tried Soul Retreat, but it does an excellent job of guiding me through the principles of meditation via my breathing whilst providing soothing stories/affirmations/nature talks to some lovely backdrops.



There is no reason not to at least try the app to see if you can benefit your mental health and well-being.

Whilst the full subscription model will help the developer continue to expand on the locations and develop the app further.



The app has opened an avenue that I never thought I would benefit from, but I was wrong.

We all could do with a little time to ourselves to reflect and clear our heads every day.




It will be a mainstay on my headset!

developer_response.body:
Burntbreadman thank you so much for this.

Hearing that you had never meditated before and it still helped you get into it is honestly one of the best compliments I can get. I tried really hard to make it feel welcoming and simple, more like taking a breather somewhere beautiful than doing a lesson.

I am really glad the guidance style worked for you too, especially the mix of breathing with stories, affirmations, and nature talks. I spend a lot of time trying to make it feel supportive rather than preachy.

And thanks for understanding the free version and the rotating environment - for anyone else reading this the free version contains 2 core environments plus another that changes each week. I genuinely appreciate anyone who uses it regularly whether they ever upgrade or not.

Thanks, Ben

reviews.review_description: Soul Retreat has a feature which makes it really special…  on any other 360 video player you can only lool around but in Soul Retreat you also can move around… and it’s a game changer

reviews.review_title: AI's meh, visuals and audio are amazing
reviews.review_description: Highly recommend downloading the free version, though a subscription is hard to recommend. I don't care for the AI myself, at best it's surface level flowery wording, and at worst it's really disjointed and robotic sounding, about what you expect from AI. That said, the scenery and audio are beautiful and do an excellent job giving you something serene to focus on and meditate with. It's not a bad idea to just turn off the AI voice and put a guided meditation on your phone while using this app if you're bothered by it like I am sometimes.

developer_response.body:
roebro thanks for the thoughtful and balanced review.

I’m really glad the environments and audio worked for you. A huge amount of effort has gone into making the places feel serene and real, so it means a lot to hear that part is doing its job.

On using your own guided audio, you can actually do that right in the headset. From the Music tab you can open the browser and play a YouTube meditation, Spotify, or whatever you like, while staying in the environment. You can also start a timed session from the Music tab, or do paced breathing with music by selecting one of the breathing options there.

On the AI, I think it is a bit personal. Some people love it, others do not. It also tends to feel more special the more you have personalised it. If you have not already, it is worth a look at Profile, then Preferences, then Personalised Guidance.

I’m continuing to refine the AI wording and how the sessions flow. On voice tone and intonation I am somewhat dependent on the AI voice provider, and I will keep upgrading as the available voice models improve.

If you have time, I’d genuinely love to hear which meditation styles or story types work best for you, and which feel weaker. If you are up for sharing, pop into the Discord and tell me what parts felt disjointed or too flowery. That kind of feedback really helps me refine it.
https://discord.gg/zSJQfxaEdk
Ben

reviews.review_description:
Love this app. So much customisation and so many options to tune out real life for a time out. 
Well done on such a visually and audibly stunning app.

Definitely enjoyed eachenvironments and everything that it had offered. Its a good app that helps bring meditation and helps relieve stress when needed.  I definitely can recommend to anyone who enjoys meditating.

developer_response.body:
HawaiianPizza2024 thanks so much for the kind review.

I am really glad you enjoyed the environments and that it helped when you needed to relieve stress. That is the whole reason I made Soul Retreat, to be an easy place you can drop into when life feels busy or stressful.

Really appreciate the recommendation too. Ben

reviews.review_description: I've been using this app for a while now, and I really love it. It's so relaxing, especially when I need a little time to unwind and de-stress. I’d definitely recommend it.

developer_response.body:
TimidBunny thanks so much for this.

I’m really glad you have been enjoying it, and that it helps when you need a bit of time to unwind and de stress. That is exactly what I hoped Soul Retreat could be, a simple place you can drop into when life feels busy or stressful.

Really appreciate the recommendation. Ben

reviews.review_description: My mom love the game

Wonderful app, the resolution in the vr environments is superb, easy to follow instructions, it would be lovely to have a selection of environments showing a variety of landscapes (not just Scotland lol) in particular forests, alpine rivers and streams, canyons, waterfalls, that would be awesome, I’m only on the 7 day trial period so the subscriptions may already have these environments??? Keep them coming!!!

developer_response.body:
catherinenorris1969 thanks for this review.

I’m really glad you are enjoying the environments and finding the instructions easy to follow.

And yes, you are right that we started off quite Scotland heavy. At launch, 7 of the first 10 environments were in Scotland, but we also had 2 in Spain and 1 in France, and we have just released another Spanish location, Acherito. During your 7 day trial you have full access to the subscriber experience, and there are currently 11 environments in total, with more coming regularly. We’re aiming to release new ones every 4 to 6 weeks.

And I completely agree on wanting more variety. Waterfalls, more rivers, more trees, and more varied landscapes are all high on our list.

Thanks again, and keep them coming is exactly the plan. Ben
